body:
# Feature request

## Summary

<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the component or enhancement that you want. -->

### aria-current="page"

When screen reader users are navigating, it is necessary to use the aria-current="page" attribute to inform them of their current location. This helps prevent them from clicking the link to the page they are already on. Currently, the aria-current attribute is not being added, so I believe this area needs enhancement.

Using a method like that of [Radix UI](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/navigation-menu%23link), which manages this via props, seems like a good approach.
Alternatively, the value prop currently only manages the open state of the Popup. If a Link is a child of NavigationMenu.Item, we could consider using the value prop to also manage whether the link is for the current page.

### Semantic HTML

A navigation element is a hierarchical structure containing a list of links. For that reason, I think it's better to use `<ul>` and `<li>` tags to clearly convey this structure to screen reader users. In fact, W3C examples also show navigation elements structured as `nav > ul > li > a`.

Of course, this is more of a best practice to consider rather than a mandatory standard. Therefore, I would like to ask for your thoughts on this topic.


## Examples in other libraries

<!-- Provide a link to examples of this component or feature implemented in other libraries (if applicable).  -->

[Radix UI](https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/navigation-menu)

- It adds the aria-current="page" attribute via an active prop.
- It is structured as `nav > ul > li > a`.

[Hero UI](https://www.heroui.com/docs/components/navbar)

- It adds the aria-current="page" attribute via an isActive prop.
- It is structured as `nav > ul > li > a`.

## Motivation

<!-- What are you trying to accomplish? Which kind of user is this for? Providing context helps us come up with a solution that is more useful in the real world -->

comments.body:
> Semantic HTML

I was intending to change this as I agree it should default to rendering a semantic list structure. You can use the `render` prop to do so currently as a workaround

> Using a method like that of [Radix UI](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://www.radix-ui.com/primitives/docs/components/navigation-menu%23link), which manages this via props, seems like a good approach.

Can you explain a bit more about this? Isn't this up to the user to specify as a prop? All the link parts accept HTML props already. Radix has docs about it though which we may need to add.

Ah, I just saw you've written this: 

> It adds the aria-current="page" attribute via an active prop.

Yeah, this would make sense to add

This fix will be available in the next npm release of Base UI.

In the meantime, you can try it out on our Canary release channel:

```sh
npm i https://pkg.pr.new/@base-ui-components/react@2526
```

labels.name: type: enhancement
labels.description: It’s an improvement, but we can’t make up our mind whether it's a bug fix or a new feature.

labels.name: has workaround
labels.description: There’s a bug, but users have a complete workaround, so no urgent fix or release is needed.

labels.name: component: navigation menu
labels.description: Changes related to the navigation menu component.

title: [navigation menu] enhance accessibility
